demographics.living_situation: lives with partner, who is present and supporting her

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The pain is blinding, but I have to speak very quietly and calmly, otherwise the hospital will just tell me I'm hysterical and send me home with nothing again.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last day my usual pelvic pain got dramatically worse — now it's agonising, I've been vomiting, I feel like I'm going to pass out, and I can't stand up or keep water down.

symptom_narrative.onset: the pain has been building over the past 24 hours on top of a long-standing problem
symptom_narrative.duration: years of chronic pelvic pain, with a severe flare in the last day
symptom_narrative.character: deep, cramping, agonising pain low in the belly and pelvis
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it has ever been — blinding, I can't move without it screaming
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: low down across both sides of my belly and over the pubic area
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: always been tied to my cycle, but this flare has been constant and unrelenting for a day
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — a heat pad and maxed-out paracetamol and ibuprofen have done nothing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving, standing up, and it just keeps getting worse on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: vomiting, can't keep fluids down, dizzy and feel faint when I try to stand, sweaty, heart feels like it's pounding
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had bad pain for years and I've been told over and over it's just anxiety or that I'm being dramatic
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound, curled up, can't stand upright or keep any fluids down

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing pelvic pain, someone once mentioned it might be endometriosis but I've never had it properly confirmed
past_medical_history.how_managed: over-the-counter paracetamol and ibuprofen, which I've been refused stronger options for

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Has felt dismissed by doctors for years and is frightened of emergency departments because she has repeatedly been labelled drug-seeking or anxious. Her partner is present and has brought a folder of her past medical records so she does not have to fight to be believed. She says she feels she has to stay artificially calm to be taken seriously.

communication_style.typical_message_style: Deliberately quiet, calm and apologetic despite obvious distress; apologises for being an inconvenience.

reluctance_topics: being perceived as exaggerating or drug-seeking

objective_data_offered.value: lying curled up on her side, visibly sweaty and pale, gripping the bedsheets, speaking in a slow deliberately calm voice
feels like she is going to faint whenever she tries to stand up
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported near-syncope; blood pressure and heart rate not measured on a device

objective_data_offered.value: vomiting and unable to keep any fluids down
her heart feels like it is pounding and racing
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective palpitations; no measured heart rate

objective_data_offered.value: partner reports she is sweaty, cannot stand without nearly collapsing, and has not been able to hold down water
heat pad on maximum; several empty boxes of over-the-counter paracetamol and ibuprofen visible
